message: Jake

Kristy
Serial Murder Case
The film crew had taken over a corner of Central Park and was shooting again today.
! You're the only one who comes here lately.
What about the actors?
Missing on trips, taking personal leave, they're really a pain.
Is the filming process going okay?
So far, there's no problem, but
if it continues like this, we're out.
Yeah.
Someone to talk to has arrived.

Today, the staff is, as always, busy with filming.
They seem to be shooting the park using extras, but is it really necessary to film that much?
It looks like only people involved are around here. After all, outsiders would just get in the way of the work.
The director is said to be a terrifying person when filming starts, but if no one shows up, he can't get angry.
His expression is a bit stern; maybe it's because he's stressed.
There’s a bit of melancholy lingering in the air.
I haven’t seen anyone but the director lately.
Yeah, even though there are supposed to be people scheduled to shoot, none of them can make it. I wonder what they’re thinking.
Are you okay?
For now, yes. I don't feel like getting angry yet.
If no one is coming, the director doesn't really need to be here.
That's what I think.
I’m waiting for someone whose arrival is unpredictable. I have to be here; it’s fine, this is my favorite place.
I’m glad you came. At least having someone to talk to helps distract me.
Is Meryl not shooting today?
No, I’d like to start filming whenever she comes.
So you haven’t seen her since that time?
Exactly. If she would just come, we could get some filming done.
I’ll let her know if I see her.
Uh-huh.
Well, it seems you’re someone I can trust, but even so, it won’t help to say anything.
Uh-huh.
Yeah.
That’s true.
She’s quite the troublesome one.
This time, the escape has been more intense than ever.
Meryl seems to have her own reasons for not wanting to come.
To say something like that means you’ve heard something from that person, right?
Yeah, sort of.
It's probably about how Jennifer and I are getting close or something.
Who knows what Meryl is thinking.
Today, we had a shoot with Jennifer.
At least Jennifer is working hard.
Yeah.
Everyone else is MIA. The lead hasn’t even made it to New York, and Guy is off traveling.
R-right.
What’s happened to everyone?
Jennifer is wonderful! She's truly fresh and shines differently!
That’s a glowing review.
Well, there’s no one else to compliment.
But isn’t that unfair to Jennifer?
Sadly, the reality is allowing her to steal the spotlight.
No joke, Jennifer is amazing. Really amazing.
Are things really that bad between her and Meryl?
That’s just what Meryl is saying, isn’t it? I think Jennifer wants to get along with her.
I see.
But according to Meryl, she was saying she’s trying to take the directing role from her.
Hey, don’t just swallow whatever she says. Meryl has a bit of a victim mentality. Poor Jennifer.
Jennifer is discussing things with me for her role preparation. She's very dedicated to her work.
So, is that what Meryl misunderstood?
Exactly. I wonder why everyone speaks ill of Jennifer. It’s sad watching from the sidelines.
Jennifer is a pure and refreshing woman.
Well, it's tough to hear that. The main cast isn't helping out at all.
I understand your feelings.
If you stumble from the start, it makes the future uncertain.
Don't worry, everything will surely go smoothly starting tomorrow.
I hope so.
Rather than praying, I'd be happier if you could find Cindy, take care of Meryl's request, and bring Guy along.
Show your true detective skills and do your best.
Any progress on the Cindy matter?
Come on, that's something I'd like to know too. I haven't heard from Gloria since then.
Is she looking for her too?
Maybe Gloria has an idea of where Cindy might have gone.
You think so too? I've been worried because Gloria doesn't seem anxious at all.
Regardless of the circumstances, I want her to handle her job properly. Isn't that just common sense?
Yes, that's true. What could have happened?
I don't want to think it's the aftermath of a lovers' quarrel.
Cindy has her own filming too.
It's been quiet since then, and I can't read what she's thinking at all.
She seems to be quite a difficult woman.
There’s also the theory that she simply hates men, but I wish she could get along with others a bit more.
I wonder if that's even possible.
I think there's a high possibility she knows where Cindy is.
Then she should just look for her on her own.
Since she made a point to tell you, it seems she doesn't have the details.
I feel bad for you. If Gloria finds Cindy first...
Your reputation will take a nosedive.
About Guy,
Yeah, that's it. When did he go on a trip without anyone knowing? So, when is he coming back?
That's what I want to know, too.
What!? You don't know either?
I'm stumped.
Hah.
I'm sorry.
No, you don't need to apologize.
I assumed you knew everything. But you're not Guy's manager, after all.
I was careless.
Guy has a habit of just up and traveling whenever trouble arises, and I think this is another case of that.
Did he get into some trouble?
There was a little article in BAM.
You shouldn't take that third-rate magazine seriously, right?
I hope Guy comes back soon.
He must be pretty stressed out with how things aren't going smoothly with filming.
I wish the filming could progress smoothly soon.
It seems like the large cast is causing disarray.
Is Meryl skipping filming because she dislikes acting with Jennifer, or is she just too distracted by her daughter?
When you become a big actress, I guess they overlook your whims.
Jennifer is hard to get a grasp on. When I met her at the party, she seemed like a prim and proper beauty.
But since I haven't seen her since, I can't really judge.
It would be nice to have a chance to talk slowly.
Seems like a tough filming situation ahead. I really hope they can complete it, but will it really be okay?
The director must be having a hard time too.
I wonder where Cindy is and what she's doing?
If the current job is focused on movies, the impact on other areas is probably minimal, but staying in this state forever won’t be good.
Even though I heard it from Gloria, I'm not sure if it's necessary to seriously look for her.
I got the story about searching for Cindy from Gloria, but it seemed like she said it reluctantly because the director told her to.
In that case, I'm torn about whether I should seriously search for Cindy or not.
Since Gloria seemed to dislike me, I’m not eager to reach out to her.
Setting off on a trip without telling the director the details...
is a bit inappropriate, isn’t it?
I thought my relationship with Guy was long-term, but perhaps our friendship isn’t as deep as I thought.
I should be going soon. Sorry for the disruption.
Come visit again.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
